LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-26-2023 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF DAVID CHEN'S MOTION TO SEAL CONFIDENTIAL PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. UNOPPOSED MOTION GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH STATES THAT THE MOTION WAS NOT OPPOSED AND WHICH RECITES THE FACTORS SET OUT IN CRC 2.550(D). JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Amended Notice Of Motion To Seal Confidential Portions Of Complaint Redacted
Off Calendar Nov-02-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. PATRICIA E. TENENBAUM, Esq. of Brown Neri Smith & Khan, LLP (Tel: 310-593-9890) for plaintiff;
Atty. SCOTT L. MENGER, Esq. of Carlton Fields, LLP (Tel: 310-843-6300) for defendants Robert M.C. Forster and Armor.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF DAVID CHEN's Motion To Seal Confidential Portions Of Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's "motion to seal confidential portions of complaint" is denied.

Court records are presumed to be open to the public and any sealing order must be narrowly tailored. (CRC 2.550.) Defendant's opposition asserted that some of the material plaintiff seeks to seal is already publicly available - a point plaintiff's reply does not dispute. The opinion plaintiff relies on - Universal City Studios, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1273, 1283 - is not to the contrary. It holds that a party seeking to seal court records must show "a substantial probability that it will be prejudiced absent closure or sealing." (Id.) Prejudice from "disclosure" of publicly available information is difficult to show, and plaintiff has not shown it.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR No. 13859, Email: justusbalentinecsr@gmail.com, Tel: 714-471-2597 (302/RBU)
